verses.text: Melchisedec, 'King of righteousness' and 'King of peace,' without recorded descent, is 'made like unto the Son of God,' a priest continually. A type. His timeless priesthood pictures Christ's.
Melchisedec, 'King of righteousness' and 'King of peace,' without recorded descent, is 'made like unto the Son of God,' a priest continually. A type. His timeless priesthood pictures Christ's.
Melchisedec, 'King of righteousness' and 'King of peace,' without recorded descent, is 'made like unto the Son of God,' a priest continually. A type. His timeless priesthood pictures Christ's.
Abraham gave tithes to Melchisedec, and 'the less is blessed of the better.' The greater priest. Melchisedec's rank surpassed even the patriarch's.
Abraham gave tithes to Melchisedec, and 'the less is blessed of the better.' The greater priest. Melchisedec's rank surpassed even the patriarch's.
Abraham gave tithes to Melchisedec, and 'the less is blessed of the better.' The greater priest. Melchisedec's rank surpassed even the patriarch's.
Abraham gave tithes to Melchisedec, and 'the less is blessed of the better.' The greater priest. Melchisedec's rank surpassed even the patriarch's.
The Levites who took tithes also paid them, in Abraham, to Melchisedec. Lesser order. Levi's priesthood bowed to a higher one.
The Levites who took tithes also paid them, in Abraham, to Melchisedec. Lesser order. Levi's priesthood bowed to a higher one.
The Levites who took tithes also paid them, in Abraham, to Melchisedec. Lesser order. Levi's priesthood bowed to a higher one.

If perfection were by the Levitical priesthood, why another priest 'after the order of Melchisedec'? Christ sprang from Judah. New priesthood. The law's priesthood could not perfect; a better was needed.

If perfection were by the Levitical priesthood, why another priest 'after the order of Melchisedec'? Christ sprang from Judah. New priesthood. The law's priesthood could not perfect; a better was needed.
A priest arises 'after the power of an endless life'; 'the law made nothing perfect,' but a better hope by which 'we draw nigh unto God.' Better hope. Christ brings what the law could not.

A priest arises 'after the power of an endless life'; 'the law made nothing perfect,' but a better hope by which 'we draw nigh unto God.' Better hope. Christ brings what the law could not.
Made priest with an oath, Jesus is 'made a surety of a better testament.' Sworn surety. God's oath secures the new and better covenant in Christ.
Made priest with an oath, Jesus is 'made a surety of a better testament.' Sworn surety. God's oath secures the new and better covenant in Christ.
Made priest with an oath, Jesus is 'made a surety of a better testament.' Sworn surety. God's oath secures the new and better covenant in Christ.
Many priests died, but He continueth ever; 'wherefore he is able... to save them to the uttermost,' ever living to intercede. Saves fully. His endless life secures our complete salvation.

Such a high priest 'became us' — 'holy, harmless, undefiled,' who offered Himself once, 'consecrated for evermore.' Perfect priest. Sinless and eternal, He needs no second offering.
